UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT RIVER PROPERTY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SEPTEMBER 11, 2012 UNAPPROVED CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS IMPROPERLY FILED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILED TO FILE AN APPROVED CROSS COMPLAINT AND FAILED TO SEEK A LEAVE TO AMEND. (OCTOBER 26, 2012 ORDER). CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S ARGUMENT REGARDING SEPTEMBER 28, 2012 STIPULATION IS UNPERSUASIVE AS IT DID NOT PREVENT CROSS-COMPLAISANT FROM ASSERTING CLAIMS AGAINST PLAINTIFF FOLLOWING DISMISSAL OF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE. REGISTERED OF ACTIONS DOES NOT INDICATE THAT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE WAS EVER DISMISSED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION FOR PREVAILING PARTY ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE "PREVAILING PARTY DETERMINATION IS TO BE MADE ONLY UPON FINAL RESOLUTION OF THE CONTRACT CLAIMS." (SEE HSU V. ABARRA (1995) 9 CAL.4TH 863, 876.) SEE ALSO CIVIL CODE 1717. WIGGINS' MOTION IS PREMATURE SINCE THERE HAS NOT BEEN A FINAL RESOLUTION OF THE CONTRACT CLAIMS BETWEEN THE PARTIES. WIGGINS' CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED SEPTEMBER 11, 2012 REMAINS OUTSTANDING, AND THERE HAS NOT BEEN A FINAL RESOLUTION OF THE CONTRACT CLAIMS BETWEEN THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEFENDANT MAY FILE A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THE AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO FEBRUARY 8, 2013. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN THAT THERE WAS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE ANY OF DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE OR RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. WHETHER PLAINTIFF WOULD HAVE EVER RESUBMITTED THE MOTION TO THE BANKRUPTCY COURT AND RECEIVED APPROVAL BY THE BANKRUPTCY COURT IS SPECULATIVE. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR AN ORDER RETURNING $10,000 TO HER IS NOT PART OF THIS ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT DID NOT MOVE FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON HER CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND THE COURT CANNOT GRANT RELIEF THAT IS DIFFERENT FROM THE GROUNDS IN THE NOTICED MOTION. A DETERMINATION OF ANY AWARDS OF ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS WILL BE HEARD UPON A PROPERLY FILED MOTION BY DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

DEFENDANT DAWN WIGGINS' Motion For Recovery Of Deposit is Off calendar. This motion was incorrectly filed in Department 302. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray K. Shahani, Esq., Attorney at Law, (650-348-1444), for River Property Trust, Cross Defendant and moving party.
Thomas P. Kelly III, Esq., Attorney at Law, (707-542-3373), APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL for Dawn Wiggins, Cross complainant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT RIVER PROPERTY TRUST's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED without leave to amend. September 11, 2012 unapproved cross-complaint was improperly filed. Cross-complainant failed to file an approved cross complaint and failed to seek a leave to amend. (October 26, 2012 order). Cross-Complainant's argument regarding September 28, 2012 Stipulation is unpersuasive as it did not prevent Cross-Complaisant from asserting claims against Plaintiff following dismissal of First American Title. Registered of Actions does not indicate that First American Title was ever dismissed. Prevailing party to prepare Order approved as to form. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Continued To Oct-11-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANT RIVER PROPERTY TRUST'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to October 11, 2013, to be heard in department 501 at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all real property related disputes. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RAY K. SHAHANI, TEL. NO. (650) 348-1444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER,

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Dawn Wiggins' motion for prevailing party attorney's fees is denied without prejudice. The "prevailing party determination is to be made only upon final resolution of the contract claims." (See Hsu v. Abarra (1995) 9 Cal.4th 863, 876.) See also Civil Code 1717. Wiggins' motion is premature since there has not been a final resolution of the contract claims between the parties. Wiggins' Cross-Complaint filed September 11, 2012 remains outstanding, and there has not been a final resolution of the contract claims between the parties. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RAY K. SHAHANI, ESQ., 650.348.1444, FOR PLAINTIFF RIVER PROPERTY TRUST.
PHOMAS P. KELLY, ESQ., 707.545.8700, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden that there was no triable issue of material fact regarding Plaintiff's breach of contract claim. Plaintiff does not dispute any of Defendant's evidence or raise a triable issue of material fact. Whether Plaintiff would have ever resubmitted the motion to the Bankruptcy Court and received approval by the Bankruptcy Court is speculative. Defendant's request for an order returning $10,000 to her is not part of this order granting summary judgment. Defendant did not move for summary judgment on her Cross-Complaint, and the Court cannot grant relief that is different from the grounds in the noticed motion. A determination of any awards of attorney fees and costs will be heard upon a properly filed motion by Defendant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint Nunc Pro Tunc
Off Calendar Oct-26-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ray K. Shahani, Esq., Tel. 650.348.1444, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court modified its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANT DAWN WIGGINS' Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross Complaint Nunc Pro Tunc is denied. The Court cannot approve of the filing of the so-called amended cross-complaint because that pleading has never been presented to the Court. Defendant may file a motion for leave to file the amended cross-complaint. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
